four_layer_diagnosis.claim_alignment: could-not-express -- C2 PE-vs-raw correlation never computed (all corr null)
four_layer_diagnosis.biological_reference: clear -- affective PE vs raw magnitude in dACC-like arbitration is biologically grounded; no divergence
four_layer_diagnosis.prerequisites: present -- post-SP-CEM main path, E2_harm_a trained in P1 for PE_FORWARD
four_layer_diagnosis.implementation: partial -- script hardcodes dacc_bias_max_abs=2.0 with weights that saturate the clip
four_layer_diagnosis.environment: adequate -- reef env, C0/C1 pass on PE_FORWARD
four_layer_diagnosis.measurement: misleading -- |bias| constant 2.0 every seed/condition -> zero variance -> Pearson undefined
four_layer_diagnosis.integration: coupled -- dACC bias path active but discriminator signal collapsed by clip

biological_reference.closest_mechanism: dACC/aIC precision-weighted prediction error vs raw harm magnitude for action biasing

targets.recommended_evidence_quality_note: V3-EXQ-597 non_contributory for MECH-258 (failure autopsy 2026-05-21). C0/C1 pass on PE_FORWARD (entropy + forward_r2) but pre-registered C2 failed vacuously: mean |dACC score_bias| == 2.0 on every seed (script sets dacc_bias_max_abs=2.0 with interaction/suppression weights that saturate the clip), so corr(|bias|, model_pe) and corr(|bias|, raw_norm) are null (zero variance). The PE-vs-raw discriminant was therefore untestable -- not evidence against precision-weighted PE routing. Retest after EXQ-597b (or equivalent) logs pre-clip bias or uses a non-saturating clip so C2 can vary across steps.
learning_extracted: Hardcoded dacc_bias_max_abs=2.0 in the 597 script can fully saturate |score_bias|, voiding a correlation-based C2
C1 forward_r2 can pass while C2 is vacuous -- do not treat as mixed MECH-258 evidence
597b should measure pre-clip bias or lower max_abs / weights before re-running the discriminant
